blocks.title: Circular Seating Arrangement - 8 Persons
blocks.explanation: To solve circular arrangements, first fix the position of one person, then arrange others relative to them. Clockwise is left and anti-clockwise is right when everyone faces the center.

concepts.definition: Arranging elements in a circle facing inward or outward.

examples.sentence: Eight people A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H are sitting around a circular table facing the center. A is sitting second to the left of B.
examples.rule: Left is clockwise and right is counter-clockwise when facing inward.
examples.working: 1. Place B at the bottom. Since everyone faces center, A is second to the left of B (clockwise), which is position 2.
